Class.Documentation:
This class contains the overall controller for the features of the set1.

So the features to update the facility manager password and to get shelf information, for one and all shelves.

It would include the {@link #updateFacilityManager(String)} , {@link #getShelf(String)} , and {@link #getShelves()} methods.

@author Rémi Séguin (@Seguin-Dynastie)
Class.Documentation_plain:
This class contains the overall controller for the features of the set1. So the features to update the facility manager password and to get shelf information, for one and all shelves. It would include the {@link #updateFacilityManager(String)} , {@link #getShelf(String)} , and {@link #getShelves()} methods.
@author Rémi Séguin (@Seguin-Dynastie)
Class.Name: CheECSEManagerFeatureSet1Controller

Operation.Documentation:
Updates the password of the facility manager.Returns an error message if invalid, or empty string if successful.The method performs validation on the new password based on the following criteria:- Password cannot be empty.- Password must be at least 4 characters long.- Password must contain at least one uppercase character.- Password must contain at least one lowercase character.- Password must contain at least one special character from !, #, or $.Then we update the password if all criteria are met, are helper messages are returned otherwise.

@author Rémi Séguin (@Seguin-Dynastie)
Operation.Documentation_plain: Updates the password of the facility manager.Returns an error message if invalid, or empty string if successful.The method performs validation on the new password based on the following criteria:- Password cannot be empty.- Password must be at least 4 characters long.- Password must contain at least one uppercase character.- Password must contain at least one lowercase character.- Password must contain at least one special character from !, #, or $.Then we update the password if all criteria are met, are helper messages are returned otherwise. @author Rémi Séguin (@Seguin-Dynastie)
Operation.Name: updateFacilityManager
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: error message if invalid, or empty string if successful
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: error message if invalid, or empty string if successful

Operation.Documentation:
Retrieves a specific shelf by its ID.Converts it into a TOShelf transfer object.First, it checks if the provided ID is valid.If valid, it retrieves the Shelf object and computes its maximum columns and rows based on its locations.Then, it populates a TOShelf object with the shelf's details, including cheese wheel information at each location.If the shelf is not found, it returns null.

@author Rémi Séguin (@Seguin-Dynastie)
Operation.Documentation_plain: Retrieves a specific shelf by its ID.Converts it into a TOShelf transfer object.First, it checks if the provided ID is valid.If valid, it retrieves the Shelf object and computes its maximum columns and rows based on its locations.Then, it populates a TOShelf object with the shelf's details, including cheese wheel information at each location.If the shelf is not found, it returns null. @author Rémi Séguin (@Seguin-Dynastie)
Operation.Name: getShelf
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: the TOShelf object representing the shelf, or null if not found
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: the TOShelf object representing the shelf, or null if not found

Operation.Documentation:
Retrieves all shelves in the system.Converts each shelf into a TOShelf transfer object.Before doing so, initializes an empty list to hold the TOShelf objects.First, it retrieves all Shelf objects from the CheECSEManager.Then, for each shelf, it computes its maximum columns and rows based on its locations.Next, it populates a TOShelf object with each shelf's details, including cheese wheel information at each location.Finally, it returns a list of all TOShelf objects.

@author Rémi Séguin (@Seguin-Dynastie)
Operation.Documentation_plain: Retrieves all shelves in the system.Converts each shelf into a TOShelf transfer object.Before doing so, initializes an empty list to hold the TOShelf objects.First, it retrieves all Shelf objects from the CheECSEManager.Then, for each shelf, it computes its maximum columns and rows based on its locations.Next, it populates a TOShelf object with each shelf's details, including cheese wheel information at each location.Finally, it returns a list of all TOShelf objects. @author Rémi Séguin (@Seguin-Dynastie)
Operation.Name: getShelves
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: list of TOShelf objects representing all shelves in the system
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: list of TOShelf objects representing all shelves in the system

Operation.Documentation:
Retrieves the facility manager from the system.

@author Daniel Yu (CrestyDY)
Operation.Documentation_plain: Retrieves the facility manager from the system. @author Daniel Yu (CrestyDY)
Operation.Name: getFacilityManager
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: the FacilityManager object, or null if no facility manager exists
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: the FacilityManager object, or null if no facility manager exists

Operation.Documentation:
Creates a new facility manager in the system.Returns an error message if invalid, or empty string if successful.The method performs validation on the email and password:- Email cannot be empty.- Password cannot be empty.- Password must be at least 4 characters long.- Password must contain at least one uppercase character.- Password must contain at least one lowercase character.- Password must contain at least one special character from !, #, or $.- A facility manager must not already exist (only one allowed).

@author Daniel Yu (@CrestyDY)
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a new facility manager in the system.Returns an error message if invalid, or empty string if successful.The method performs validation on the email and password:- Email cannot be empty.- Password cannot be empty.- Password must be at least 4 characters long.- Password must contain at least one uppercase character.- Password must contain at least one lowercase character.- Password must contain at least one special character from !, #, or $.- A facility manager must not already exist (only one allowed). @author Daniel Yu (@CrestyDY)
Operation.Name: createFacilityManager
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: error message if invalid, or empty string if successful
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: error message if invalid, or empty string if successful

Class.Documentation:
Controller class for managing the Purchase section of the application.

It handles navigation between different pages related to purchases,

such as displaying, adding, and updating purchase records.

@author Remi Seguin
Class.Documentation_plain:
Controller class for managing the Purchase section of the application. It handles navigation between different pages related to purchases, such as displaying, adding, and updating purchase records.
@author Remi Seguin
Class.Name: PurchaseController

Class.Documentation:
Controller class for displaying and managing Purchases in the application.

It provides functionality to view a list of purchases, search through them,

and view detailed information in a popup.

@author Remi Seguin
Class.Documentation_plain:
Controller class for displaying and managing Purchases in the application. It provides functionality to view a list of purchases, search through them, and view detailed information in a popup.
@author Remi Seguin
Class.Name: PurchaseDisplayController

Class.Documentation:
Controller class for the Purchase Form in the CheECSEManager application.

This class handles the user interactions and data processing for creating a new purchase of cheese wheels.

@author Remi Seguin
Class.Name: PurchaseFormController

Operation.Documentation:
Refreshes the robot action log display by fetching the latest log from the backend.Formats the log for better readability with each action on a new line.If the log is empty or unavailable, displays a helpful message to the user.This method ensures execution on the JavaFX application thread.

@author Daniel Yu
Operation.Documentation_plain: Refreshes the robot action log display by fetching the latest log from the backend.Formats the log for better readability with each action on a new line.If the log is empty or unavailable, displays a helpful message to the user.This method ensures execution on the JavaFX application thread. @author Daniel Yu
Operation.Name: refreshActionLog

Operation.Documentation:
Allows other controllers to safely request a refresh of the robot UI and action log.This is a static method that can be called from anywhere in the application.The refresh will be executed on the JavaFX application thread to ensure thread safety.If no robot controller instance exists, the request is silently ignored.

@author Daniel Yu
Operation.Documentation_plain: Allows other controllers to safely request a refresh of the robot UI and action log.This is a static method that can be called from anywhere in the application.The refresh will be executed on the JavaFX application thread to ensure thread safety.If no robot controller instance exists, the request is silently ignored. @author Daniel Yu
Operation.Name: requestRefreshRobot

Operation.Documentation:
Triggers the robot to perform treatment on all cheese wheels in a specific purchase.Uses the selected maturation period from the dropdown.This method:1. Validates the purchase ID selection2. Validates the maturation period selection3. Calls the backend controller to perform treatment on all eligible cheese wheels4. Displays success or error messages to the user5. Refreshes the robot status and action logOnly cheese wheels that are:- Not spoiled- Match the maturation period- Assigned to shelf locationswill be treated by the robot.

@author Daniel Yu
Operation.Documentation_plain: Triggers the robot to perform treatment on all cheese wheels in a specific purchase.Uses the selected maturation period from the dropdown.This method:1. Validates the purchase ID selection2. Validates the maturation period selection3. Calls the backend controller to perform treatment on all eligible cheese wheels4. Displays success or error messages to the user5. Refreshes the robot status and action logOnly cheese wheels that are:- Not spoiled- Match the maturation period- Assigned to shelf locationswill be treated by the robot. @author Daniel Yu
Operation.Name: triggerTreatment

After:
{"licencePlate": "XYZ123","inRepairShop": false}
